observations.recommendation:
We reiterated out prior year’s audit recommendation:
That the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer evaluate and determine the cause/s of the discrepancy and record the necessary adjustments in their books. Henceforth, both officials shall reconcile their records regularly, at least on a quarterly basis, and adjustments, if any, shall be promptly and appropriately recorded; and
On the immediate monitoring, review and retrieval of records of the four non-moving/dormant accounts for possible return to source agencies or closure and transfer of balances to active accounts and other possible remedies to avoid unnecessary depletion of the funds due to bank-imposed charges
We also recommend that the Municipal Treasurer to always maintain cashbook for each account.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Accounting Unit and Property/Supplies Unit to:
Maintain complete and accurate LCs and PCs, respectively, for each class of PPE;
Perform periodic reconciliation of the LCs and PCs and ensure that any discrepancy is immediately investigated, cleared and reconciled; and
Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to take up adjustments, if any, to ensure correctness of the accounting records.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that:
Management instruct the GSO/Property Officer and concerned accountable officers/end-user offices/departments to conduct the inventory of all supplies and materials in their custody at the end of the year and to prepare the corresponding report certified correct by the Inventory Committee; and
The GSO/Property Officer maintain supplies stock cards and for the Accounting and Property Offices to reconcile their records and take up the necessary adjustments to ensure that the Inventory accounts are properly stated in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace and analyze the transactions to reconcile the difference of ₱4,190,693.75 in the reciprocal accounts Due to/Due from Other Funds and prepare the necessary adjustments to reflect the correct balances of the accounts as of December 31, 2021.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer promptly record in her cash book the collections and deposit daily and the Collecting Officers properly and completely record necessary/required data/information in the cash book to facilitate tracking, monitoring, evaluation review and reconciliation.
We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer consistently maintain printed/hard copy of updated, reconciled, footed, closed and certified cashbook.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that Management direct:
The Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the original disbursement vouchers (DV) and its supporting documents, for the preparation of the Journal Voucher/s following the procedures set forth in the NGAS Manual for LGUs;
The Municipal Accountant to review the long outstanding stale checks and take up the necessary entry reversing to Cash-in Bank and Accounts Payable the amounts thereof; and
Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the payees of unclaimed checks at least one month before a check becomes stale.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management require:
The Municipal Treasurer to identify the specific sources of funds and establish whether these were essentially idle funds in accordance with Section 21 and 22 of COA Circular No. 92-382. In cases when there are idle funds identified, investing such should have prior authority of the Sanggunian and the approval of the Local Chief Executive; and
The Municipal Treasurer to transfer the amount of ₱353,219.46 to the Municipality’s Current Account maintained for Trust Funds and hasten the implementation of the programs, projects and activities for which the fund was created/intended for.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management:
Direct the preparation and issuance of demand letters to concerned officers and employees with outstanding cash advances requiring them to immediately settle their unliquidated cash advances;
Cause or order the withholding of the payment of any amount due the officers or employees concerned with unliquidated cash advances as provided by paragraph 9.3.2 of COA Circular No. 97-002 in case of failure to settle their cash advances after demand has been made; and
Request for an Authority to Write-off the dormant receivable accounts amounting to ₱90,310.26 following the procedures provided in Sections 8.2 and 8.3 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Assessor and Property Officer to facilitate the titling of the 40 parcels of land. Relative thereto, require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds for the payment of transfer taxes and other fees so that issuance of the titles could be facilitated.
We also recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct a complete inventory taking of all PPEs of the LGU, including its real properties, and submit a copy of the RPCPPE to the Audit Team for its timely verification. Further, the Municipal Accounting Office and the Supply/Property Officer shall maintain complete and updated Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, for the real properties.

For the deficiencies noted, we recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with the Municipal Engineering Office in determining the actual status of all reported on-going projects and to effect the necessary adjustment in the books of accounts based on the result of validation in order to reflect the accurate balance of the CIP accounts;
Inquire in writing with the Department of Agriculture on the status of the abandoned project “Rehabilitation of Sula-Mataas Farm to Market Road” for coordinated courses of action for possible completion or disposal, whichever is warranted; and
Observe proper planning and monitoring of project implementation to ensure that infrastructure projects are completed on time.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive require the Property Officer to immediately dispose of the unserviceable properties in accordance with existing regulations.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that the LCE direct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to return the unutilized balance of PDAF amounting to ₱6,442,985.42 to the National Treasury pursuant to the SC Decisions.

We recommended that the Accountant prepare and submit the Monthly Trial Balance, Quarterly FS and Annual FS in the prescribed format, together with the complete set of schedules and annexes on or before the prescribed timelines in accordance with the abovementioned rules and regulations.
We also reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that Management consider hiring additional qualified personnel to the Accounting Office and to allow the personnel of the Accounting Office to attend work related trainings/seminars to equip them with the required knowledge and skills and improve work performance.

We recommended that Management provide the Auditor copies of perfected contracts for infrastructure projects and its complete supporting documents within five working days from execution thereof to allow for a systematic, timely and effective review process.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that the BAC require the contractors to submit evidence of payment of the Documentary Stamp Tax (DST) pertaining to the Performance Bond for the six infrastructure projects.  Henceforth, ensure that the correct amount of DST is paid on the Performance Bond submitted by the winning contractor before the signing of the project contract.
We recommended that Management require the BAC to strictly comply with the posting requirements of the Municipalities procurement activities pursuant to the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that Management refrain from issuing the Certificate of Acceptance simultaneously with the Certificate of Completion, disregarding the prescribed defects liability period where the City is financially protected from structural defects and failures, instead, shall issue the Certificate of Final Acceptance on completed projects after the one year defects liability period, after all defects/deficiencies, if any, shall have been corrected by the Contractor to the satisfaction of the Procuring Entity and upon the submission of the Warranty Certificate by the Contractor pursuant to Section 62.2.3 of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive inform the Office of the Supervising Auditor and the Audit Team Leader within ten days after the award of the infrastructure project or before the start of the program/activity of any postings of project signboards and/or public notice for validation by the Audit Team.

We recommended that Management, henceforth, instruct the BAC to adopt the required forms of the Philippine Bidding Documents to be provided to the prospective bidders as prescribed in the GPPB Circular No. 04-2020 and ensure that all the mandatory provisions are stated in the bidding documents submitted by the bidders upon evaluation thereof.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that Management direct the concerned offices to hasten the implementation of the partially and unimplemented PPAs funded by the current and prior years’ continuing appropriations to fully attain the desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes essential to the promotion of the general welfare of its constituents.

We reiterated our prior year audit recommendation that the Municipal Planning and Development Office furnish the Regional Offices of the DILG and DBM copies of the Annual Investment Program immediately after its approval to enable the oversight agencies to create/maintain the necessary database and to monitor the utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund.

We reiterated our prior year’s audit recommendation that Management:
Direct the OIC-Municipal Accountant to secure the lacking supporting documents to the DVs from the concerned offices/personnel and submit promptly to the Audit Team, otherwise, the non-submission thereof would warrant the issuance of a Notice of Suspension/Disallowance; and
Require strict compliance by the OIC-Municipal Accountant with the pertinent provisions of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 to protect the interest of the government and prevent unwarranted disbursements of public funds.

We recommended that Management henceforth:
Require the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRM Fund, and submit the report on or before the 15th day after the end of each month, through the LDRRMC and LDC, to the COA Auditor and furnish the same to the OCD and DILG-LGOO; and
Instruct the DRRM Officer to provide copy of the approved LDRRMFIP, and as required in Section 5.1.6 of COA Circular No. 2012-002.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal DRRM Officer, in coordination with the Municipal Budget Officer and the Municipal Accountant, to incorporate in the LDRRMFIP list of all the PPAs to be funded out of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF of previous years, for approval of the LDRRM Council in accordance with RA No. 10121, NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1 and COA Circular No. 2012-002 and furnish the same to the Audit Team.

We recommended  that Management, through MDRRMO, ensure the efficient and effective utilization of the DRRMF thru implementation of programs and projects designed to avoid, lessen or limit the potential adverse impact of disasters, such as projects pertaining to disaster prevention, mitigation and preparedness.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant to prepare a Journal Entry Voucher to take up the adjusting entries to transfer the unexpended balance of the QRF and the DRRMF-MOOE to the TF books under the account Trust Liability-DRRMF.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive advise the Municipal Accountant to maintain a Subsidiary Ledger (SL) for the transfer of the unutilized DRRMF to the Special Trust Fund by year of transfer, receipts of NDRRMF by transferring agency, DRRMF from other LGUs by LGU and other sources by Donor as mandated in the above-cited regulations.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant include in the Notes to Financial Statement a disclosure of the amount and details of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF as required by Section 5.1.16 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that Management require the LSB to formulate/prepare the required Three-Year Strategic Program and consider priorities indicated in the School Investment Plan (SIP) and the Division Education Development Plan (DEDP) to serve as reference in the preparation of the SEF budget. Henceforth, strictly follow the required processes and documents in planning and budgeting for SEF under the revised guidelines on the use of SEF.
Further, we recommended that LSB prepare PPAs in accordance with DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 1, s. 2017 and No. 2, s. 2020, particularly on expenditures allowed to be charged against the SEF, in accordance with Section 2 of the cited Joint Circular No. 2, s. 2020 and furnish the Audit Team with documents that supported the formulation of plans and programs chargeable to SEF for verification/evaluation.
Furthermore, we recommended that Management require complete, timely and accurate reports, data and information as basis in the preparation of budget estimates for the use of SEF.
We commended the management for exerting extra effort in successfully complying the requirements set by the DENR-EMB-NSWC for the approval of the Ten-year Solid Waste Management Plan.

We recommended that Management adhere to the provisions stipulated in RA No. 9003 by requiring concerned officials/employees and the SWM Board the preparation of an annual SWM plan/budget and accomplishment/progress report on the strategies implemented at the end of the year for submission to the NSWC copy furnished the Audit Team.
We likewise recommended that Management provide the Audit Team copy of executive order creating the Management SWM Board

We recommended that the LCE spearhead the creation of the plantilla position of MENRO and its staff.
Likewise, require the MENRO to exercise and discharge its functions and responsibilities as are necessary, appropriate or incidental to efficiently and effectively provide basic services and facilities such as solid waste disposal system or environmental management system and services of facilities related to general hygiene, as stated in Section 17 of the Local Government Code of 1991.

We recommended that Management require the Office of the Senior Citizen to conduct a general assembly for the purpose of selecting three (3) persons to be nominated as OSCA Head and submit list of nomination to the Office of the Mayor. From the list of nominees, the LCE shall appoint an OSCA Head which shall serve for three (3) years. Provide a copy of appointment/designation to the appointee and to other concerned offices for easy reference. Henceforth, strictly adhere to the prescribed guidelines in the nomination, selection and appointment of the OSCA Head.

We recommended that Management direct the submission of final and reconciled LCPC budget for CY 2021, approved by majority of the Council, endorsed to, and approved by the Sangguniang Bayan. Henceforth, we recommend the regular and proper coordination between concerned offices for the complete and accurate reporting of LCPC plans and budget.

We recommended that Management require the concerned officials/emplouees:
To maintain and update relevant data/information/document needed in identification and quantification of the targeted beneficiaries in the preparation of plan and budget for LCPC; and
To prepare accomplishment report as directed in DILG MC No. 2021-039 for monitoring, evaluation and decision making, copy furnished the Audit Team for verification.

We recommended Management direct the Municipal Accounting Office to maintain separate subsidiary ledgers for LCPC and the Municipal Budget Office to indicate the specific fund in the OBR. Also, require the regular reconcilation of balances between the two offices to ensure consistency, accuracy, and completeness of financial records.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the Municipality through the DILG LGOO establish an IMTF for proper monitoring and evaluation of the LCPC functionality and submit the required report to the Provincial IMTF, copy furnished the DILG.

We recommended that Management:
Submit the required supporting documents to validate payments of COVID-19 SRA totaling ₱3.32 million; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursements of funds are properly supported with the complete required supporting documents to avoid the issuance of Notice of Suspension/Notice of Disallowance, if warranted.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that Management:
Adopt prescribed control procedures in the receipt, inventory and issuance of Donations- In Kind, and record the proper cost of the ending inventory based on the value declared by the donor, if available, or using the prevailing market price of the donated items;
Require the concerned offices/units to properly categorize the Donations- in Kind, received by the LGU and intended for COVID-19 response, be reported in the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances for consolidation; and
Instruct the LDRRMO or the personnel in-charge to submit a consolidated report on Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances, separately for Cash and In-Kind transactions, to the NDRRMC through the OCD, copy furnished the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that Management direct:
The Municipal Accountant to conduct analysis and reconciliation of the remaining unremitted mandatory deductions pertaining to the December 31, 2021 balances, if any, and to remit, thereafter, to the concerned agencies;
Henceforth, she shall ensure that any unremitted balance of mandatory deductions at the end of each month shall be analyzed to ensure that the same are immediately reconciled and remitted; and
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to ensure the timely remittance of the mandatory deductions to the concerned government agencies at the end of each month.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Assessor and Property Officer to facilitate the titling of the 40 parcels of land. Relative thereto, require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds for the payment of transfer taxes and other fees so that issuance of the titles could be facilitated.

We also recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct a complete inventory taking of all PPEs of the LGU, including its real properties, and submit a copy of the RPCPPE to the Audit Team for its timely verification. Further, the Municipal Accounting Office and the Supply/Property Officer shall maintain complete and updated Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, for the real properties.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Accounting Unit and Property/Supplies Unit to:
Maintain complete and accurate Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, for each class of PPE;
Perform periodic reconciliation of the Ledger Cards and Property Cards and ensure that any discrepancy is immediately investigated, cleared and reconciled; and
Prepare the necessary adjusting journal entry to take up adjustments, if any, to ensure correctness of the accounting and property records.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Assessor and Property Officer to facilitate the titling of the 40 parcels of land. Relative thereto, require the Municipal Budget Officer to appropriate funds for the payment of transfer taxes and other fees so that issuance of the titles could be facilitated.
We also recommended that the Inventory Committee conduct a complete inventory taking of all PPEs of the LGU, including its real properties, and submit a copy of the RPCPPE to the Audit Team for its timely verification. Further, the Municipal Accounting Office and the Supply/Property Officer shall maintain complete and updated Real Property Ledger Cards and Property Cards, respectively, for the real properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer evaluate and determine the cause/s of the discrepancy and record the necessary adjustments in their books. Henceforth, both officials shall reconcile their records regularly, at least on a quarterly basis, and adjustments, if any, shall be promptly recorded in the appropriate records.
We also recommended the immediate monitoring, review and retrieval of records of the four non-moving/dormant accounts for possible return to source agencies or closure and transfer of balances to active accounts and other possible remedies to avoid unnecessary depletion of the funds due to bank-imposed charges.

We recommended that Management direct:
The Municipal Accountant to review the long outstanding stale checks and take up the necessary entry/ies to correct the balances of the accounts Cash-in Bank and Accounts Payable;
The Municipal Treasurer to cancel the unclaimed stale checks and furnish the Municipal Accountant with the original disbursement vouchers (DV) and its supporting documents, for the preparation of the Journal Voucher/s following the procedures set forth in the NGAS Manual for LGUs; and
Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the payees of unclaimed checks at least one month before a check becomes stale.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant promptly take up necessary adjusting entries for the noted book reconciling items to reflect the correct balances of pertinent accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to hasten the preparation of the monthly Bank Reconciliation Statements and submit to the Audit Team a copy thereof, together with the corresponding Journal Entry Vouchers (JEVs)/paid checks/original copies of Debit and Credit Memos for review, on or before the deadline; and
Further, instruct the Municipal Accountant to secure snapshot copies of the transactions with the depository bank, in lieu of bank statements, to facilitate the preparation of the Bank Reconciliation Statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer:
Deposit intact all collections remitted to her by the collectors with the authorized depository bank on the same day or not later than the next banking day in compliance with existing regulations;
Promptly record in her cashbook the deposit/s of collection/s for easier tracing, monitoring and review; and
Strictly observe the relevant guidelines on the proper preparation of the Report of Collection and Deposits to prevent confusion in the recording of the Cash-on Hand and ensure the accuracy and reliability of the RCDs and the Cashbooks.

We recommended that Management instruct the Property Officer and concerned accountable officers/end-user offices/departments to conduct the physical inventory of all supplies and materials in their custody at the end of each semester and to prepare the corresponding report certified correct by the Inventory Committee. Thereafter, submit copies thereof to the MA for reconciliation, copy furnished the Audit Team.
We also recommended that the GSO/Property Officer maintain supplies stock cards and for the Municipal Accounting and Property Offices to reconcile their records and take up the necessary adjustments to ensure that the Inventory accounts are properly stated in the financial statements.

We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer transfer the amount of ₱9.18 million from the General Fund to the Trust Fund-NDRRMF bank account and furnish the Municipal Accountant copy of the pertinent debit and credit memos.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary journal entries transferring the said amount from the Due to NGAs under the General Fund books to the Trust Liability- NDRRMF in the Trust Fund books.

We reiterated our prior year’s recommendation that the LCE direct the MT and MA to return the unutilized balance of PDAF amounting to ₱6.44 million to the National Treasury pursuant to the SC Decisions.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant submit the Subsidiary Schedules of General Ledger accounts, including the Aging of Receivables and Payables accounts, as soon as possible to facilitate the exigent audit of the Financial Statements.
Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant prepare and submit the Monthly Trial Balance and Quarterly FS, together with Schedules and Annexes on or before the prescribed timelines in accordance with the abovementioned rules and regulations.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the GAD guidelines prescribed under PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01 to ensure that GAD funds are properly utilized to achieve its purpose of gender equality and women empowerment.

We recommended that, henceforth, the BAC require the winning bidder to submit a Contractor’s All Risk Insurance (CARI) upon award of the contract for infrastructure projects. In case of failure to submit the required insurance, the BAC shall strictly implement the prescribed sanctions against the erring contractor.

We recommended that Management require the Bids and Award Committee (BAC) to strictly comply with the posting requirements of the Municipalities procurement activities pursuant to the Revised IRR of R.A. No. 9184.

We recommended that the BAC require the contractor to submit evidence of payment of the Documentary Stamp Tax (DST) pertaining to the Performance Bond for the project Construction of Sanitary Landfill (Phase I). Henceforth, the BAC ensure that the correct amount of DST is paid on the Performance Bond submitted by the winning contractor before the signing of the contract for any project.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management require the LSB to formulate/prepare the required Three-Year Strategic Program and consider the recommendations of the DepED PSDS and SDS as well as priorities indicated in the Schools Investment Plan (SIPs) and Division Education Development Plan (DEDPs) to serve as reference in the preparation of the SEF budget.
Henceforth, the LSB strictly follow the required processes and documents in planning and budgeting for SEF under the revised guidelines on the use of SEF pursuant to DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular (JC) No. 1, s. 2017.

We recommended that the Local School Board strictly observe the timely preparation and submission of the required reports to the concerned oversight bodies and posting of the same in the Municipality’s website to promote transparency and accountability for the Special Education Fund.

We recommended that Management direct the concerned offices to hasten the implementation of the partially and unimplemented PPAs funded by the current and prior years’ continuing appropriations to fully attain the desired socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes essential to the promotion of the general welfare of its constituents.

We recommended that the Municipal Planning and Development Office furnish the Regional Offices of the DILG and DBM copies of the Annual Investment Program immediately after its approval to enable the oversight agencies to create/maintain the necessary database and to monitor the utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Secure the lacking supporting documents from the concerned offices/personnel and submit promptly to the Auditor, otherwise, the non-submission thereof would warrant the issuance of a Notice of Suspension/Disallowance; and
Strictly comply with the pertinent provisions of P.D. No. 1445 and COA Circular No. 2012-001 to protect the interest of the government and prevent unwarranted disbursements of public funds.

We recommended that Management prepare and submit the necessary reports enumerated in Item No. 1, Part II of DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2012-120 before the end of the year, to serve as basis in the LCPC budget preparation to ensure that the actual priority programs have been identified and funded for the effective utilization thereof.

We recommended that the Municipality, through the DILG LGOO, establish an IMTF for proper monitoring and evaluation of the LCPC functionality and submit the required report to the Provincial IMTF, copy furnished the DILG.
We also recommended that Management refer to the DILG issuance on the “New Comprehensive Guidelines for the Establishment, Strengthening and Monitoring of the LCPC at All Levels and for Other Purposes” which takes effect in CY 2021 for proper guidance.

We recommended that Management strictly adhere to the guidelines on government grants received by the Municipality to ensure its proper and efficient utilization in the implementation of  intended priority to programs, projects and activities.

We recommended that the LCE require the Local Finance Committee to submit the Monthly Reports on Fund Utilizations and Status of Implementation of PPAs on BGCM of the Municipality to the House of Representatives, Senate of the Philippines, House Committee on Appropriations, and Senate Committee on Finance, as required in Item 4 of DBM LBC No. 125.

We recommended that Management:
Adopt prescribed control procedures in the receipt, inventory and issuance of Donations- In Kind, and record the proper cost of the ending inventory based on the value declared by the donor, if available, or using the prevailing market price of the donated items;
Require the concerned offices/units to properly categorize the Donations- in Kind, received by the LGU and intended for COVID-19 response, be reported in the Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances for consolidation; and
Instruct the MDRRMO or the personnel in-charge to submit a consolidated report on Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances, separately for Cash and In-Kind transactions, to the NDRRMC through the OCD, copy furnished the Auditor, as required in COA Circular 2012-009.

We recommended that Management, henceforth, strictly abide with the terms and conditions stated in the Memorandum of Agreement, through the immediate distribution of subsidies to identified eligible beneficiaries, to achieve the objectives of the Emergency Subsidy Program of the government.

We recommended that, henceforth, the LCE require the MSWD Officer to strictly observe the proper, complete and timely preparation and submission of data/reports to pertinent government agency/ies to provide ample time for the required deduplication process to effectively meet the objectives of the programs.

We recommended that Management require the BAC to revise the consolidated Annual Procurement Plan whenever there are additional programs, projects and activities included in the updated PPMPs submitted by the different departments/offices of the LGU.

We recommended that, henceforth, Management require the BAC to prepare the Procurement Monitoring Report, Agency Procurement Compliance and Performance Indicator and the Annual Procurement Plan, and ensure strict compliance on the submission thereof to the GPPB as required.

We recommended that Management require the MSWD Officer to submit the required Report on the Provision of Assistance to the Relocated Informal Settler Families in Resettlement Communities in response to COVID-19 emergency to the DILG Regional Office, and henceforth, strictly comply with the reporting requirements set in the DILG Memorandum Circular.

We recommended that the LCE compel the MSWD Officer to require the concerned Punong Barangays to submit to the LGU the list of beneficiaries, including persons with disabilities provided with assistance such as food and health services during COVID-19 pandemic, and prepare a consolidated report for submission to the Office of the Provincial Governor of Albay, as required in DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2020-066.
